name: Morello
classification_notes: The three subfamilies — Pen, Pencil and Marker — indicate a hand-drawn casual script family, each variant evoking a different writing instrument.
summary: A casual hand-drawn script family offered in three instrument-themed variants: Pen, Pencil and Marker.
description: Morello comes in three subfamilies, each mimicking a different drawing tool — Pen, Pencil and Marker — giving designers a choice of texture and weight for hand-lettered work. The family suits display use, packaging and branding where an informal, handcrafted quality is needed. Little technical detail is disclosed on the product page beyond the trio of variants.
foundry_blurb: Old City Mailroom offer Morello as a trio of script fonts — Morello Pen, Morello Pencil and Morello Marker — each available to purchase separately from their font shop.

name: Index Mono
classification_notes: The typeface is explicitly named and sold as a mono font, with a companion Index Sans Mono, placing it clearly in the monospaced category with a sans-serif structure.
summary: A monospaced typeface from Old City Mailroom, sold alongside a companion Index Sans Mono variant.
description: Index Mono is a monospaced typeface available at least in a Thin weight, with a companion family called Index Sans Mono sold separately. The page provides very limited detail beyond a type tester and purchase options. Its pairing with a distinct sans mono variant suggests a considered approach to the mono genre, though specifics of the design remain unclear from the available copy.
foundry_blurb: Old City Mailroom offers Index Mono for purchase alongside Index Sans Mono, presented via an interactive type tester on their site.

name: Swim Club
classification_notes: Swim Club offers four stylistic variants spanning wide and tall cuts in both sans and slab forms, positioning it as a display family built for expressive, texture-driven work rather than body text.
summary: A four-variant display family from Old City Mailroom offering wide and tall cuts in both sans and slab forms, with uppercase settings producing a textured effect distinct from the solid lowercase.
description: Swim Club is structured around two proportional extremes, wide and tall, each available in sans and slab variants. Uppercase characters generate an open, textured appearance while lowercase renders solid forms, giving designers control over density and visual weight within the same typeface. The family suits signage, branding and editorial work where a relaxed, characterful display voice is needed.
foundry_blurb: Old City Mailroom note that typing in uppercase produces a textured effect while lowercase yields solid letterforms, distinguishing Swim Club's four subfamilies across wide and tall proportions in both sans and slab styles.

name: Prairie
classification_notes: The family centres on Prairie Script, a display script face, with a decorative stitched variant and a complementary sans companion, placing the primary classification firmly in script/display territory.
summary: A display script family with a decorative stitched variant and a paired sans, evoking handcrafted Americana.
description: Prairie is a three-part family built around a display script, a stitched decorative variant of that script, and a companion sans. The stitched cut adds an embroidered or folk-craft texture to the letterforms, giving the family a handmade, rural American character. The sans provides a functional counterpart for supporting text alongside the more expressive script styles.
foundry_blurb: Old City Mailroom offer Prairie as a family comprising Prairie Script, Prairie Stitched and Prairie Sans, each available to purchase separately via their type tester.

name: Mailroom
classification_notes: The family includes a Romance variant and a Glyphs cut alongside standard weights, suggesting a decorative, expressive display typeface rather than a text workhorse.
summary: A display family from Old City Mailroom with a standard and Romance variant, built around expressive letterforms and a bespoke glyph set.
description: The family ships in Light and Regular weights, with a Romance subfamily in the same two weights and a standalone Glyphs cut for ornamental use. The combination of a Romance variant and a dedicated Glyphs font suggests a typeface oriented towards editorial, branding or packaging work that calls for personality over neutrality. Little technical detail is available on the page, but the structure points to a boutique display offering with decorative ambitions.
foundry_blurb: Old City Mailroom offers Mailroom in Light and Regular weights, alongside Mailroom Romance in Light and Regular, and a separate Mailroom Glyphs font, all available to try and purchase directly from the foundry's site.
